skill.name: gws-slides
skill.description: Google Slides: Read and write presentations.
skill.summary: Create, read, and update Google Slides presentations through the Google Workspace CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All 23 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly treated markdown documentation as executable code. The SKILL.md file contains only usage instructions and CLI command examples for the Google Workspace Slides tool. There is no executable code, no network calls, no filesystem operations, and no security-sensitive patterns. The skill is a legitimate productivity tool from the Google Workspace CLI repository.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks (```bash) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples showing CLI usage patterns for gws slides commands, not actual shell execution. All 13 locations are within markdown code fence blocks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear false positive - markdown code fences (```bash) contain documentation examples, not executable shell commands. No backtick execution exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reference in API Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 26 as Python HTTP library usage. This line contains the description of the Google Slides batchUpdate API method in documentation text. No Python or HTTP library code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear false positive - line 26 is prose describing the batchUpdate API method, not Python HTTP code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Path in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged ../gws-shared/SKILL.md on line 16 as a path traversal attack. This is a standard relative path reference in markdown linking to a shared authentication skill file, not an attack vector.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear false positive - markdown reference to sibling documentation file, standard skill dependency pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged multiple lines as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. The SKILL.md file contains no cryptographic code whatsoever. These appear to be spurious detections triggered by markdown content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear false positive - no cryptographic code exists in this markdown documentation file. Purely spurious detections.

content.value_statement: Creating and editing Google Slides presentations manually is time-consuming. This skill automates presentation workflows through the Google Workspace CLI so you can build, update, and manage slides programmatically.
actual_capabilities: Create new Google Slides presentations with custom titles
Read and retrieve existing presentations and their content
Apply batch updates to slides with multiple operations
Manage individual pages and slide elements
Discover available API methods and schema information through CLI help
limitations: Requires the gws CLI tool to be installed and authenticated
Depends on Google Workspace API access and proper OAuth configuration
Cannot perform actions outside the Google Slides API capabilities
Requires reading the shared gws-auth skill for authentication setup

use_cases.title: Automated report presentations
use_cases.description: Generate recurring business presentations from data sources. Create standardized slide decks for weekly reports, monthly reviews, or quarterly summaries with consistent formatting.

use_cases.title: Bulk slide content updates
use_cases.description: Update text, images, or charts across multiple presentations simultaneously. Apply branding changes or template updates to an entire library of existing decks.

use_cases.title: Training material generation
use_cases.description: Build onboarding presentations and training decks from structured content. Convert documentation, runbooks, or process guides into formatted slide presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google Slides presentation titled Q4 Sales Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: You need a blank presentation to start building

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve an existing presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the latest version of my presentation with ID 1BxANqGM8v6kTzBfZz4qW9x3eR7yU2pLm5nH8jK1oA0
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: You want to view or modify an existing deck

prompt_templates.title: Batch update slide content
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the presentation with ID 1BxANqGM8v6kTzBfZz4qW9x3eR7yU2pLm5nH8jK1oA0. Replace the title on slide 1 with Annual Results and add a new text box on slide 2 that says Key Findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: You need to make multiple changes to a presentation

prompt_templates.title: Build a presentation from structured data
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a presentation titled Monthly Metrics Report. Then use batchUpdate to add three slides: one with a title slide layout, one with a bullet-point summary, and one with a blank layout for a chart. Use gws schema to check the required parameters for each update request.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: You are building a complete presentation programmatically from data

output_examples.input: Create a new presentation titled Team Update
output_examples.output: A new blank Google Slides presentation is created with the title Team Update. The presentation ID is returned for future reference.
The full presentation structure is returned, including slide IDs, page elements, layouts, and master references. You can inspect titles, content, and formatting of each slide.

best_practices: Always run gws schema before building batchUpdate requests to verify required parameters and data types
Use the presentations.get method to inspect existing presentations before making changes to avoid overwriting content
Test batchUpdate requests with a single update first, then add more operations once the format is confirmed
anti_patterns: Do not call batchUpdate with unverified parameters - each request must pass validation or the entire batch will fail
Do not skip reading the shared gws-auth skill - proper authentication is required before any API call
Do not hardcode presentation IDs in workflows - retrieve them dynamically or store them in configuration

faq.question: What do I need before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the gws CLI tool installed and authenticated with your Google Workspace account. Read the shared gws-auth skill for authentication setup instructions.

faq.question: How do I discover available commands?
faq.answer: Run gws slides --help to browse available resources and methods. Use gws schema to inspect the parameters, types, and defaults for any specific method.

faq.question: What happens if a batchUpdate request fails?
faq.answer: The entire batch is validated before any updates are applied. If any individual request is invalid, the entire batch fails and no changes are made. Fix the invalid requests and retry.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to edit existing slide content?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the presentations.get method to retrieve the current presentation, then use batchUpdate to modify specific elements. You need the slide and element IDs to target changes.

faq.question: How do I specify a custom presentation ID?
faq.answer: When creating a presentation with the create method, include a presentationId in the request parameters. If omitted, Google Slides generates a unique ID automatically.

faq.question: Does this skill support images and charts in slides?
faq.answer: The gws CLI provides access to the Google Slides API. Check gws schema for specific resource methods to see supported operations for images, charts, tables, and other slide elements.
